es.exampleTranslation: On average I sleep seven hours.
es.regionNote: Spain: 'de media'. LatAm: 'en promedio'. Served form should follow the reader's region if we ever localise.

it.exampleTranslation: On average I sleep seven hours.

bridge.note: One phrase, three prepositions: Italian in media, Mexican Spanish en promedio, Peninsular Spanish de media. The Atlantic runs straight through this entry — which is exactly why regions is a required field.

interference.it_to_es: Regional split: LatAm says en promedio; Spain says DE media — a preposition flip inside Spanish itself.

review.notes: From Joe's Italian teacher's prepositions crash course, 2026-07-16. ES pairing chosen by Claude, unverified — reviewer to confirm. Cue drafted by Claude 2026-07-20 from the entry's meaning and bridge — English situational framing, not a language claim, so it needs editorial taste rather than native review. Override freely.

cue: How do you give an average?
